title: Place-of-supply derivation for all 7 Fulfilment variants (TRACT-FULF-01, TRACT-FULF-02)
description: §4.3's derivation table, applied to every Fulfilment variant from TRACT-WIRE-VEC-09, with a constant seller establishment and buyer residence held across every case specifically so a reader can see the anchor is NEVER either of those two values except in the two variants §4.3 says should equal buyer residence. The 'ship' case additionally demonstrates TRACT-FULF-02: the anchor is bound only once the buyer's fulfilment choice (the one destination out of the offer's served list) is recorded on the order, per §4.8.

cases.name: ship — anchor is the ORDER's chosen destination, not the offer's whole destination list
cases.note: §4.3: ship -> delivery destination. The Fulfilment object lists destinations SERVED (['FR', 'DE', 'ZA']); the actual anchor is whichever ONE the buyer chose on the order (§4.8, TRACT-FULF-02) — here 'FR'. Seller establishment (ZA) and buyer residence (DE) play no role.

cases.name: collect — anchor is the stated place
cases.note: §4.3: collect -> the stated place. PlaceRef.country = 'IT', independent of seller (ZA) and buyer (DE).

cases.name: digital-grant — anchor is buyer residence
cases.note: §4.3: digital-grant -> buyer residence ('nothing physical happens anywhere'). Anchor = 'DE'.

cases.name: perform-at-place — THE §0.1/§4.3/§11.2 forcing example, verbatim (TRACT-FULF-01)
cases.note: The exact case TRACT-FULF-01 and §4.3 both cite: an event held in country C ('FR', the venue), sold by a seller established in country A ('ZA'), to a buyer resident in country B ('DE'). Only the venue answers the place-of-supply question; neither party's country does. Anchor = 'FR', matching neither A nor B.

cases.name: perform-remote — anchor is buyer residence
cases.note: §4.3: perform-remote -> buyer residence ('no physical venue to anchor to'). Anchor = 'DE'.

cases.name: access-grant, place named — anchor is that place
cases.note: §4.3: access-grant -> the stated place, if named. Anchor = 'GB'.

cases.name: access-grant, no place named — anchor is buyer residence
cases.note: §4.3: access-grant -> buyer residence, otherwise. Anchor = 'DE'. Same Fulfilment variant (key 5) as the previous case, opposite anchor — 'the field name is shared by two economically different cases, and the anchor has to follow whichever one this instance actually is' (§4.3).

cases.name: return-required — anchor is the stated place
cases.note: §4.3: return-required -> the stated place ('the same reasoning as collect — the item changes hands there'). Anchor = 'JP'; the term (30 days, key 7) does not affect the anchor.
